description: Read a specific pod in a given namespace by its name using the Kubernetes API.

evaluation.validation_summary: Ctx2Skill-lite preview verifier contract. External corpus parse/audit keeps this ephemeral until a human approves import.

implementation.prompt_template: To read a pod named {name} in namespace {namespace}, send a GET request to /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods/{name}. Optionally include a request_id header.

pod.description: The full Pod object as returned by the API

preconditions: Valid Kubernetes API credentials are available
The target namespace and pod exist

name: kubernetes_read_pod_api_doc_kubernetes_read_pod

challenges.expected_evidence: A valid HTTP GET request to /api/v1/namespaces/production/pods/nginx
challenges.failure_signal: Wrong HTTP method (e.g., POST) or incorrect path
rubric: Constructs the correct URL with namespace 'production' and name 'nginx'
Does not include unnecessary headers or parameters
challenges.task: Read a pod named 'nginx' in namespace 'production' using the Kubernetes API.

challenges.expected_evidence: GET request to /api/v1/namespaces/default/pods/my-pod with header request_id: abc-123
challenges.failure_signal: Missing header or incorrect header name/value
rubric: Includes the request_id header with correct value
challenges.task: Read a pod named 'my-pod' in namespace 'default' and include an optional request_id header with value 'abc-123'.

challenges.expected_evidence: GET request to /api/v1/namespaces/dev/pods/test and ability to parse the response
challenges.failure_signal: Error in request construction or inability to handle response
rubric: Assumes successful response with pod data
challenges.task: Read a pod named 'test' in namespace 'dev' and handle the response (assuming the pod exists).

constraints: The API is read-only; it does not modify resources.
Authentication and authorization are required but not specified in the excerpt.
Only a single pod object is returned.
facts: The endpoint GET /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods/{name} reads a specific pod.
An optional request_id header can be included.
procedures: To read a pod, send a GET request to /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods/{name} with path parameters namespace and name.
Optionally include a request_id header for tracking.
context_pack.summary: Focused Kubernetes API reference for reading a pod by name in a namespace.

selected_candidate.prompt_template: To read a pod named {name} in namespace {namespace}, send a GET request to /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods/{name}. Optionally include a request_id header.

ctx2skill_evidence.selected_reason: The candidate skill directly maps to the single API operation in the context, is atomic, and can be reused for any pod read request. It covers all necessary parameters and optional headers, making it the strongest and most focused skill for the given API documentation.
